title: Professional Relationships, Community Participation, and Consent · Guided Evidence Sprint
summary: A guided career studio extends reciprocal relationship practice plan while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reciprocal relationship practice plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.
Make a bounded, easy-to-decline request for information, feedback, introduction, or context with purpose and time expectations.
Contribute relevant help, documentation, testing, facilitation, learning notes, or community work within actual competence and norms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Network Map
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Network Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Network Map, keep Network Map connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Relationship Integrity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity.
steps.why: Retrieving Relationship Integrity through the concrete reciprocal relationship practice plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity, keep Relationship Integrity connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Relationship Integrity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Relationship Integrity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Automating personalized outreach is harmless if response rates improve.
